name: Crispy Oven Sweet Potato Fries (Smoky)
description: Oven-baked sweet potato fries that actually crisp, seasoned smoky to go with fried chicken and served with a chipotle-lime mayo. Adapted from The Mediterranean Dish (za'atar swapped for a smoked-paprika blend).
recipeIngredient: 3 medium sweet potatoes, cut into 1/4-inch sticks
For the chipotle-lime mayo: 1/2 cup mayonnaise; 1-2 chipotle peppers in adobo, minced, plus 1 tsp adobo sauce; juice of 1/2 lime; 1 small garlic clove, grated; pinch of salt
recipeInstructions: Cut the sweet potatoes into even 1/4-inch sticks, place in a large bowl, and cover with cold water. Soak at room temperature 30 minutes (or refrigerate overnight) to draw out surface starch. Drain, then dry the bowl well.
Preheat the oven to 425°F and line a large sheet pan with parchment.

Pat the fries thoroughly dry. For an extra-crisp shell, toss them with 1 tablespoon cornstarch first. Return to the dry bowl, drizzle with 2-3 tablespoons olive oil, and toss to coat. Combine the smoked paprika, garlic powder, and cayenne; add and toss to coat evenly.

Spread the fries on the pan in a single layer, spaced apart and not touching (use two pans if needed — crowding steams them soft). Bake 15 minutes, flip every fry, then bake 15-20 minutes more until crisp and golden.
Off the heat, season with kosher salt. Rest 5 minutes on a cooling rack (not the hot pan) so steam escapes.
Chipotle-lime mayo: stir together the mayonnaise, minced chipotle plus adobo, lime juice, grated garlic, and a pinch of salt. Serve alongside for dipping.
author: Suzy Karadsheh / The Mediterranean Dish

ai.cliffNotes: Oven sweet potato fries that crisp instead of flop: a cold-water soak strips surface starch, then high heat and an uncrowded single layer finish the job. Smoky-seasoned for fried chicken, with a chipotle-lime mayo.
keyTips: Soak the cut fries in cold water 30 min (or overnight) to wash off surface starch — the author's testing found this is the single biggest factor for crisp vs. floppy fries. Then dry the fries AND the bowl completely; leftover water turns to steam and sogs them.
Don't crowd the pan. Fries that touch steam each other limp — spread them in one layer with space between, using two sheet pans if you have to.
Flip every fry at the 15-minute mark; turning them really does make the difference for even browning.
For an extra-crunchy crust, toss the dried fries with ~1 Tbsp cornstarch before the oil — it bakes into a thin crisp shell.
Cut to an even 1/4 inch; thick sticks go tender, not crisp. Salt AFTER baking — salting raw pulls out moisture as they roast.
Rest 5 minutes on a cooling rack, not the hot pan, so trapped steam escapes instead of softening the bottoms.
